title: Gnocchi
subtitle: with Dilly-Fried Cabbage & Apple Arugula Salad

steps.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil for the gnocchi. Peel and thinly slice the onion. Pick and chop the dill fronds. Halve and juice the lemon. Thinly slice the apple and add to a medium bowl with the baby arugula.

Melt the butter in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Once melted, add sliced onion and cabbage and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned and softened, 5 to 7 minutes. Add smoked paprika and 1 tsp salt, stir, and reduce heat to low.

Add the gnocchi to the boiling water and cook until they float to the surface, 3 to 4 minutes. Drain, and add boiled gnocchi to the skillet with the cabbage. Cook, stirring occasionally, until browned in places, 4 to 6 minutes.

Add just half the chopped dill and a pinch of pepper to the gnocchi. Taste, and add salt and pepper as necessary. Add remaining chopped dill, just half the lemon juice, sour cream, and a pinch of salt to a small bowl and combine.

Add 2 tsp olive oil, remaining lemon juice, and a pinch of salt and pepper to the apple arugula salad. Divide the gnocchi with dilly-fried cabbage between large plates and top with dill sour cream. Serve with apple arugula salad. Dig in!

tips: * This ingredient will be split between steps.
TIP: The dill fronds are the wispy green leaves on the thick stems.
TIP: While cooking the vegetables, reduce heat as necessary to prevent burning.

Global Sensation: Cabbage is a big part of some of our favorite sides and condiments. Can you imagine a world without coleslaw, kimchi, or sauerkraut? Neither can we! We also love it fried, sautéed, and steamed. Bring on the cabbage! Not only is it tasty, it's also a nutrient powerhouse. A single serving of cabbage contains nearly half the recommended daily dose of Vitamin C, not to mention high levels of manganese, iron, vitamin B6, and fiber.
